source.title: Orexin in rostral hotspot of nucleus accumbens enhances sucrose 'liking' and intake but scopolamine in caudal shell shifts 'liking' toward 'disgust' and 'fear'

confidence_rationale: Direct causal experiment (microinjection of orexin-A into NAc shell in rats) demonstrating that orexin amplifies both 'wanting' (motivated intake) at all NAc shell sites and 'liking' (hedonic orofacial reactions) at a specific rostral hotspot. This is a clean test of the bridge between an orexin signal and downstream drive->goal-seeding behaviour, which is the SD-037 commitment that the override authority gates the drive->z_goal seeding pathway. Source quality high (Neuropsychopharmacology, Berridge lab). Mapping fidelity moderate-high: the experiment manipulates orexin tone directly at a downstream consumer node (NAc shell), so the causal chain matches the SD-037 'override_signal reweights/permits goal-seeding' architecture. Confidence held below 0.85 because the paper measures hedonic and consummatory reactions, not the full SD-037 commit-gate reweighting of z_harm under simultaneous threat.
failure_signatures: If orexin tone in downstream consumers were neutral with respect to drive-driven goal seeking, SD-037's drive->goal seeding bridge would be unsupported. Castro et al. show the opposite: orexin causally amplifies motivated intake.

The hotspot/coldspot dissociation (rostral hedonic enhancement vs uniform 'wanting' enhancement) implies that override effects are not spatially uniform; SD-037's scalar override_signal is a simplification.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Orexin-A microinjection into nucleus accumbens medial shell amplifies hedonic 'liking' reactions to sucrose at a rostral hotspot identical to the opioid hedonic hotspot, and enhances 'wanting' (palatable food intake) at all NAc shell sites. Effects are dissociable from cholinergic-dependent fear/disgust modulation in the caudal shell.
mapping.ree_translation: SD-037 commits to an override_signal that gates the SD-012 drive->z_goal seeding bridge: when override is recruited, drive_level is permitted to seed z_goal directly. Castro et al. provide a causal demonstration of the underlying biology -- orexin tone at a downstream consumer node (NAc shell) is sufficient to amplify motivated approach to food. The 'wanting' enhancement at all shell sites maps to the SD-037 drive-seeding bridge; the 'liking' enhancement at the rostral hotspot is an additional hedonic axis not currently in SD-037 but consistent with bivalent override effects.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The experiment uses appetitive sucrose with no concurrent threat manipulation. SD-037's central architectural commitment is that override REWEIGHTS z_harm at commit gates AND gates drive->goal seeding -- the harm reweighting axis is not directly tested here. Also, microinjection is supraphysiological tone delivery, not endogenous override recruitment under sustained drive+threat as in SD-037.
mapping.source_context: Microinjection experiment in adult rats (n approximately 4-8 per condition by within-subjects sucrose-quinine taste reactivity testing), with orexin-A and scopolamine targeted at sub-regions of NAc medial shell. Behavioural readouts: orofacial reactions to sucrose/quinine, intake of palatable chocolates, defensive treading.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (Berridge lab, peer-reviewed Neuropsychopharmacology, established taste-reactivity methodology). Mapping fidelity moderate-high: directly tests the drive-gating axis of SD-037 but not the harm-reweighting axis. Transfer risk moderate -- rat NAc shell microinjection generalises reasonably to human/REE drive-seeding architecture but not without caveats.
